---
title: "assertive communication worksheet"
format: html
---

## Assertive Communication Worksheet

Assertive communication helps you set boundaries and ask for what you need while staying respectful. This reduces resentment, conflict buildup, and high-risk emotional overload.

Passive communication often leads to silence and stress. Aggressive communication can damage trust and support. Assertive communication is clear, direct, and calm.

Interpersonal stress is a common relapse risk. Communication habits, emotional reactivity, and body language all affect how quickly conflict escalates and how supported you feel afterward.

When communication becomes clearer and more regulated, stress drops and problem-solving improves. These skills protect recovery by reducing isolation, resentment, and impulsive reactions during difficult interactions.

::: callout
### Instructions
Use the template below to practice one conversation you need to have.
:::

When ____________________, I feel ____________________.  
I need / request ____________________.  

| Situation | Passive response | Aggressive response | Assertive response |
|---|---|---|---|
| | | | |
| | | | |
| | | | |
